header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization & Talent Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ confidential leave cases, using evidence-based insights to refute cost assumptions
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Consult with executive leadership to redesign accountability structures and clarify cross-functional roles in support of a 2-year strategic plan
Facilitate listening sessions with 25 global stakeholders, surfacing learning gap insights to co-design 3 tailored programs
Develop process documentation, digital resource libraries, and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, cutting onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, increasing program understanding and driving 78% enrollment rate
Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ projects, utilizing feedback analysis to optimize operational strategies and vendor performance
Analyzed 2,000+ employee survey data, surfacing insights and key engagement drivers to recommend C-suite actions
Led manager effectiveness initiative, translating data into an executive-ready presentation to inform training strategy
Managed weekly SharePoint dashboards for people metrics, Net Promoter Score, and survey insights to drive employee recognition
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects in survey development, performance review, and L&D
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Streamlined onboarding across 3 global sites; interviewed stakeholders to clarify responsibilities and map new team workflows
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to other 23 global locations
Supported 5 cross-functional HR projects in talent development, succession, and Employee Relations; upheld alignment and confidentiality
Managed cross-functional HR operations spanning recruitment, compensation analysis, and performance management globally
Coordinated end-to-end international talent acquisition, including job descriptions, postings, and global compensation benchmarking
Drove a 566% increase in applications in 2 months through personalized global outreach and talent engagement
Planned and executed recruiting events and C-suite panels at top 3 universities, engaging 230+ students and generating 80+ applicants
